commit.message:
Make FCollection's set field transient to skip redundant serialization (#9991)

The set duplicates all list elements during Java serialization. Since
the list is the source of truth, rebuild the set from it in readObject()
instead of serializing it. Also fixes HashMap bucket corruption on
deserialization of circular object graphs.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Event-Planner module: private event planning with social features

Features:
- Event dashboard with "Meine Events" and "Einladungen" tabs
- Event creation: Titel, Datum, Ort, Kategorie, Beschreibung, Coverbild
- RSVP system: 3-button interface (Ja/Vielleicht/Nein)
- Participant lists: grouped by status (Zusagen/Vielleicht/Absagen)
- Event-Chat: comment thread with timestamps
- Share links: one-click sharing (event.app/event/[id])
- Event management: edit, delete, category badges
- localStorage persistence (all event data + comments)
- Responsive grid layout with event cards
- Modal-based workflows consistent with existing modules

MVP Features Delivered (as per plan):
✅ Step 1: UI Scaffold (dashboard, empty states, modals)
✅ Step 2: Core CRUD (create, read, update, delete events)
✅ Step 3: RSVP System (status buttons, participant lists)
✅ Step 4: Comments/Chat (thread, timestamps, emoji ready)
✅ Step 5: Invitations (share links, guest preview)
✅ Step 6: Polish (responsive design, error handling, XSS prevention)

New files:
- js/event-planner.js: Complete event planner module (~620 lines)
  * Dashboard with metrics (upcoming events, RSVPs, total events)
  * Event grid view with hover effects
  * Event creation modal (7 fields + category selection)
  * Event detail modal with full feature set
  * Comment system with localStorage persistence
  * RSVP button logic with visual feedback

Extended files:
- index.html: Added Events nav item (calendar icon) + script include
- css/style.css: ~80 lines of event-specific components
  * .events-grid responsive layout
  * .event-card hover effects
  * .event-hero category emoji display
  * Status badges (yes/maybe/no)
  * Participant groups styling
  * Comment styling
  * Mobile responsive breakpoints

Data Model:
- Events array stored in localStorage (key: careeross_events)
- Each event contains: id, title, datetime, location, category, members[], comments[]
- Comments stored separately per event (key: careeross_event_[eventId]_comments)
- Members track: userId, name, status (yes/no/maybe), joinedAt

Tech Notes:
- Reuses App.data, App.utils, App.modal, App.toast from existing app.js
- Compatible with existing sidebar navigation + hash router
- Consistent theming with copper + dark ink
- No external dependencies (vanilla JS + CSS)

Future enhancements (Phase 2):
- Polls/voting
- Task lists (who brings what)
- Cost splitting
- Photo gallery
- Countdown timers
- Event templates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix IDX Anomaly: 403/404/429 errors, add yfinance fallback, cache UMA

HTTP layer (utils/http.py):
- Upgrade User-Agent to modern Chrome browser string + full browser headers
- Retry on HTTP 429/5xx (was only retrying Timeout/ConnectionError)
- Parse Retry-After header on 429 to respect rate-limit window
- Clear error messages include HTTP status code and URL

UMA scraper (providers/uma_idx.py):
- Add browser Accept/Referer/Sec-Fetch headers to avoid 403
- Try IDX AJAX JSON API first (/umbraco/Surface/NewsAjax/GetNewsAjax)
- Fall back to HTML scrape with browser headers; gracefully return []
- 1-hour TTL cache (cachetools) — prevents hammering IDX on every request

Price providers:
- New providers/price_yfinance.py — zero-config, works for all IDX .JK tickers
- price_ohlc.py: auto-falls-back to yfinance on 404/403 (wrong URL / no key)
- Default provider changed from "ohlcdev" to "yfinance" in config.yml + config.py

Fundamentals (providers/fundamentals_finnhub.py):
- Try bare symbol (BBCA), IDX:BBCA, BEI:BBCA formats — IDX stocks need prefix
- Auto-fall-back to yfinance .info (bookValue / priceToBook) on any failure
- 4-hour TTL cache — fundamentals don't change intraday

Routes (routes/anomaly.py):
- UMA fetch now uses shared 1-hour TTL cache across all requests
- GET /api/uma?refresh=1 forces cache invalidation
- /api/uma returns 200 with empty list on failure (UI handles gracefully)

All 40 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix single-workflow AI generation and harden all AI endpoints

- Fix generate-ai-workflow endpoint: fetch workflow data from DB instead of
  relying on empty client params (root cause of silent generation failure)
- Increase max_tokens from 4096 to 8192 across all AI generation endpoints
  to prevent response truncation on complex workflows
- Add parseAIJsonArray helper with markdown code block stripping and
  truncated JSON repair (bracket counting + trailing comma fix)
- Increase Vercel function timeout from 60s to 300s for AI generation
- Increase client-side AI request timeout from 60s to 180s
- Fix SPA routing: use routes with filesystem handler instead of rewrites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Response Analyzer v2 at /analyze-v2/

New app expanding the original Response Analyzer with Phase 1 features:
- Table sorting (all columns, with directional arrows + tooltips)
- Session persistence via IndexedDB (24h, debounced writes)
- Mass phrase upload (.txt/.csv) in Phrase Search
- Response Viewer slide-over panel with phrase highlighting, platform
  color badges, and pagination (20/page with load more)
- Eye icon on breakdown rows (per-prompt + per-platform/stage/country)
  opens viewer pre-filtered to that data slice with context subtitle
- Pivot selector ("View by:") in both Top Phrases and Phrase Search
  to aggregate by platform, stage, or country

Also updates build.sh, vercel.json (/analyze-v2/ routes), and
apps/home/index.html (new app card).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Technology/Repository relationship (#204)

* Add CPE field to Repository model

Allows storing the CPE string of the technology a repository was researched
for, enabling statelessly queryable OSINT results by technology CPE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace CPE field with HAS_REPOSITORY relationship

Reverts the CPE field on Repository in favor of a HAS_REPOSITORY
relationship type that links Technology → Repository in the graph.
This supports many-to-many associations between technologies and
repositories for OSINT research.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Public field to Repository model

Allows repositories to be marked as public, which disables credential
requirements for cloning. Used by Constantine OSINT to scan public
repositories without needing GitHub/GitLab credentials.

commit.message:
Fix flights endpoint crash by adding private-jet to Flight model Literal type

The Tribune Private Jet feature added flights with class "private-jet" to
flights.json, but the Flight Pydantic model only allowed economy,
premium-economy, business, and first. This caused a validation error that
crashed the entire /api/flights endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restructure /events page — Header outside midnight theme wrapper

The Header was rendering inside the data-theme="midnight" wrapper,
which applies CSS resets (border:0, padding:0 on buttons, color:inherit
on links) that conflict with header.css styles. This caused scroll
locking and broken layout on mobile.

Restructured to match homepage (LandingPage.tsx) pattern:
- Layout is now a minimal passthrough (just imports CSS)
- Page fetches branding and passes to client component
- Client component renders Header OUTSIDE data-theme wrapper
- Branding CSS vars injected into both :root and wrapper
- Branding JSON serialized for useBranding() hydration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full-stack Databricks App for real-time machine risk monitoring.
- FastAPI backend with async SQL warehouse + Lakebase integration
- React + Tailwind v4 frontend with risk dashboard
- AI-powered risk summaries via Foundation Model API
- Real-time signal simulation and health scoring
- Unity Catalog integration for machine telemetry data

commit.message:
Desktop v3.5.1: fast parallel CDP port detection

- Add cdp_utils.py: parallel TCP scan using ThreadPoolExecutor
  (socket connect_ex for instant port-open checks, then HTTP query
  only on open ports)
- Detection time: ~1s preferred port hit, ~2.7s full scan
  (was up to 48s before)
- All scripts (daemon, inject, GUI) use fast scanner with fallback

commit.message:
feat: Phase 2A — call center, agent, and call reason analytics

New tables: numbers_dim, groups_dim, tags_dim, call_tags,
call_center_daily_stats, agent_daily_stats, call_reasons_daily.

Calls are grouped by CloudTalk queue group (ponorna številke) with
SLO/HR split via country_code on the number dimension. Tags are
captured as a separate analytics dimension (23 known reason tags).
Agent login time omitted — not available via API.

124/124 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: migrate from ChatCompletion API to Messages API (#425)

* feat: migrate from ChatCompletion API to Messages API (amessages)

Switch all LLM calls from acompletion (ChatCompletion format) to amessages
(Anthropic Messages API format) using any-llm-sdk >= 1.10. This is a
big-bang migration touching all 5 call sites: agent loop, compaction,
heartbeat, vision, and startup verification.

Key changes:
- System prompt extracted to `system` kwarg instead of messages list
- Tool schemas use `{name, description, input_schema}` format
- Tool inputs delivered as parsed dicts (no more JSON string parsing)
- Response parsing uses MessageResponse content blocks
- Usage tracking uses input_tokens/output_tokens directly
- Removed json_repair dependency (tool inputs are always dicts)
- Removed defensive getattr/isinstance checks where Messages API
  types guarantee the fields exist

Fixes #422

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: make estimate integration test more deterministic

Use explicit system prompt, directive user message, and temperature=0
to reduce flakiness from Haiku not calling the generate_estimate tool.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove `parameters` field from Tool, make `params_model` required

Every production tool already uses params_model and the registry enforced
this at runtime. The raw parameters dict existed only as a backward-compat
fallback for tests. Removing it eliminates dual-path logic in
tool_to_function_schema, _validate_tool_args, and _summarize_tool_params,
and lets the type system enforce what the registry previously checked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace raw COMPOSE_MESSAGE_TOOL dict with Pydantic model

Extract params_to_input_schema() helper from tool_to_function_schema()
so non-agent-loop callers can generate schemas from a Pydantic model.
Replace the 30-line hand-written tool schema dict with ComposeMessageParams
and use model_validate() instead of manual dict.get() parsing. Non-numeric
priority values now fall back to no_action instead of silently defaulting.

* refactor: remove params_to_input_schema, use model_json_schema directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(voice): add N-party mixing, cross-rate resampling, and MixerProvider ABC

Phase 2 of the audio bridge plan:

- N-party additive mixing (mixing_strategy="mix"): each participant
  hears a mix of all other participants, never their own audio.
  2-source sum, 3+ source averaging with int16 clipping.

- Cross-rate resampling: bridge resamples frames when source and target
  sessions have different sample rates (e.g. 8kHz SIP to 48kHz WebRTC),
  both in direct-forward and mix strategies.

- MixerProvider ABC with two implementations:
  - PythonMixerProvider: pure-Python struct-based, zero dependencies
  - NumpyMixerProvider: vectorized int32 accumulation, ~20x faster
  Auto-detection: NumPy if available, Python fallback. Lazy exports
  for NumpyMixerProvider at all levels (mixer/, pipeline/, voice/, roomkit/).

- AudioBridgeConfig.enabled check (was dead code)
- Cached resampler singleton (was allocating per frame)

- 57 bridge tests: 20 core, 10 VoiceChannel integration, 14 mixer
  unit tests (parametrized Python+NumPy), 7 N-party, 6 cross-rate

- Two examples:
  - voice_bridge_summary.py: mock 3-party conference with AI summary
  - voice_sip_bridge_summary.py: real SIP bridge with Deepgram STT,
    Anthropic Claude summary, auto language detection (multi)

feat: add 6 Python vulnerability audit skills from GHSA data

- injection (CWE-77/78/89/94/95/917): 7 cases
- path-traversal (CWE-22/23): 7 cases
- auth-bypass (CWE-285/287/863): 7 cases
- deserialization (CWE-502): 8 cases
- ssrf (CWE-918): 7 cases
- xss (CWE-79): 8 cases

Each skill contains Detection Strategy (Source/Sink/Sanitization model),
Detection Checklist, False Positive Guide, and real-world cases with
verified patch diffs from 471 high-severity Python vulns (CVSS>=8.0,
2023-2026).

Also includes project scaffolding: fetch scripts, templates, and
the main SKILL.md for ghsa-skill-builder itself.

commit.message:
Warm-up state machine on startup by replaying recent session bars

- Add StockScanner.warm_up(): replays last N regular-session bars per
  symbol without emitting alerts, so the state machine reaches the
  correct current state (TRENDING/PULLBACK) on startup.
- Extract _step() from process() to share state-transition logic between
  normal scan ticks and warm-up replay.
- Replace initial _scan_cycle() call in ScannerThread.run() with
  _warm_up_cycle() which calls warm_up() instead of process().
